name: baoyu-post-to-x
description: Post content and articles to X (Twitter). Supports regular posts with images and X Articles (long-form Markdown). Uses real Chrome with CDP to bypass anti-automation.
capabilities: Post regular text tweets with up to 4 images (PNG, JPEG, GIF, WebP)
Publish long-form Markdown articles to X (X Premium required)
Convert Markdown to HTML with frontmatter support
Use real Chrome browser with CDP to bypass anti-automation detection
Copy images to system clipboard for pasting into posts
Send real paste keystrokes via osascript to bypass synthetic event detection
Save Chrome login sessions for persistent authentication
Preview posts before publishing with --submit flag
author: JimLiu
securitySummary: AI analysis failed after multiple attempts - MANUAL REVIEW REQUIRED before publishing. This skill cannot be auto-published until reviewed by a human.
valueStatement: An AI skill for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code
limitations: Requires Google Chrome or Chromium installed on the system
Requires bun runtime to execute TypeScript scripts
First run requires manual login to X (session cookies are saved afterward)
X Articles feature requires an active X Premium subscription
Image paste on macOS requires Terminal accessibility permissions
Code blocks in Markdown render as blockquotes (X doesn't support code blocks)

useCases.title: Share AI-generated content to Twitter
useCases.description: Post Claude-generated insights, summaries, or announcements directly to X with supporting images.

useCases.title: Publish blog posts as X Articles
useCases.description: Convert Markdown blog posts into formatted X Articles with cover images and embedded content.

useCases.title: Automated social media workflows
useCases.description: Integrate into Claude-powered automation pipelines for scheduled or triggered posting.

promptTemplates.title: Post a regular tweet
promptTemplates.scenario: Posting a regular text tweet, optionally with images

promptTemplates.title: Publish an X Article from Markdown
promptTemplates.prompt: Publish this Markdown article to X: ${SKILL_DIR}/scripts/x-article.ts ${markdown_file} --submit
promptTemplates.scenario: Publishing a long-form article to X

bestPractices: Always use preview mode first (without --submit) to verify content before posting
Set X_BROWSER_CHROME_PATH environment variable if Chrome is not in the default location
Keep Chrome window visible during image paste operations
Test the clipboard copy script separately if image paste fails
Grant Terminal accessibility permissions on macOS before attempting image posts
Use persistent Chrome profile to avoid re-logging in on each run
antiPatterns: Don't post without previewing first - always verify the composition
Don't ignore rate limiting - wait between posts if getting errors
Don't use unsupported image formats (only PNG, JPEG, GIF, WebP)
Don't attempt to post without proper Chrome/Chromium installation
Don't forget to add --submit flag when ready to publish

faq.question: Does this work on Windows and Linux?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill supports macOS, Linux, and Windows. On macOS, image paste uses osascript for real keystrokes. On Linux and Windows, different paste mechanisms are used.

faq.question: Do I need X Premium to use this skill?
faq.answer: X Premium is only required for the X Articles (long-form publishing) feature. Regular tweets can be posted without a Premium subscription.

faq.question: How does this bypass X's anti-automation detection?
faq.answer: The skill uses real Chrome browser with Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP) and the --disable-blink-features=AutomationControlled flag. Images are pasted using real system keystrokes (via osascript on macOS) rather than synthetic events that websites can detect.

faq.question: What happens during the first run?
faq.answer: On first run, Chrome opens with X.com - you must log in manually. Your login session and cookies are saved to the Chrome profile directory and will be used for subsequent runs.
